user_message: Working on Wide World Importers's migration architecture. Got the email thread -- help me run the hygiene check.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-01-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:06:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:20:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-N trap (Follow-up + decision-log + MSX-proposal as separate artifacts creates context-sw); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-N failure mode (Follow-up + decision-log + MSX-proposal as separate artifacts creates context-sw)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-N -- Follow-up + decision-log + MSX-proposal as separate artifacts creates context-sw
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
